metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline submission included both required artifacts and a conservative HOLD/needs_review gate decision. Grader awarded partial credit (0.75) because the memo correctly documented embedded-packet values, multiplicative remediation calculations, and explicit caveats about missing truth thresholds, but did not enumerate all remediation combinations (pairwise and all-three) with total costs and did not provide outputs that allow programmatic verification of workbook formulas. The workbook preview could not be parsed (openpyxl unavailable) which limited formula verification.

planned_changes: Add a Recommendation worksheet (and mirror summary in the memo) that enumerates all remediation combinations: single options (O1,O2,O3), all pairwise combinations (O1+O2, O1+O3, O2+O3), and the all-three combination (O1+O2+O3). For each combination show: total_cost, budget_ok (boolean), and the per-record adjusted values after applying the combination.

Compute and display run_permission_by_record for R1-R3 or explicitly document why run_permission cannot be computed. If truth thresholds remain missing, include a clear machine-readable field in the memo and Recommendation sheet such as run_permission_by_record: omitted_due_to_missing_thresholds and indicate what thresholds are required.

Preserve spreadsheet formulas (no hard-coded numeric results) in the Calculation sheet and reference those cells from Recommendation. In the memo, include explicit cell references (e.g., Recommendation!B4 -> Calculation!C12) so verifiers can validate formulas without needing to re-run calculations.

Provide a formula-export or plain-text listing of key formulas (or a CSV of Calculation sheet with formulas) alongside the xlsx so automated graders can inspect formula integrity even if openpyxl is unavailable in the preview environment.

If any combination changes the gate decision (i.e., would produce all-true run_permission_by_record), highlight the least-cost combination that achieves a permissive outcome and show residual risk assumptions.
Re-run deterministic prechecks and include an explicit statement in the memo that the workbook preserves formulas (not values) and where to find the key formula cells.
revision_reason: Baseline submission included both required artifacts and a conservative HOLD/needs_review gate decision. Grader awarded partial credit (0.75) because the memo correctly documented embedded-packet values, multiplicative remediation calculations, and explicit caveats about missing truth thresholds, but did not enumerate all remediation combinations (pairwise and all-three) with total costs and did not provide outputs that allow programmatic verification of workbook formulas. The workbook preview could not be parsed (openpyxl unavailable) which limited formula verification.
